p.seg: Let present and future know that I, Robert de Bernham, have given, granted and by this my present charter made firm to God and Blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in free and everlasting alms, the net in Berwickstream upon the Tweed, which Master David my brother, then chamberlain of the lord king, purchased from Ralph de Hauville, falconer of the lord king, and gave it to Roger my brother and to his heirs or his assignees. From this net the same Roger endowed Christiana daughter of Adam the Marshal, his wife, for her lifetime, after whose death the net shall belong to me by reight of heritage. For the said monks to hold and have as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as the charter of the said Ralph de Hauville, made more fully thereupon for the said Master David, bears witness. I and my heirs shall entirely warrant and defend the said net to the said monks against all people.
In testimony of this matter I, for myself and for my heirs, have applied my seal to this written deed.
As witness, the community of Berwick.
